payload.answer: The prior state was “planned/open.” The new information says the MVP is implemented, so the effective status should move from open to implemented (at least for the MVP). Keep the original planning/open fact as historical context, but treat the implementation as the latest state.
payload.decision: Update status: the previously planned “open” state is superseded by the new fact that the MVP has been implemented (status transitioned to implemented). Do not delete the plan; record the implementation as the latest status.

entity_resolution.reason: Both statements refer to the same project item (“Brain Slack agent”); the new input adds a concrete milestone completion (MVP implemented) that changes the effective status.

payload.intent: Explain and resolve the status transition conflict for the “Brain Slack agent” project item.

memory_cards.statement: The Brain Slack agent was planned with status set to open.
The Brain Slack agent MVP has been implemented.
Because the MVP is implemented, the overall status can be treated as transitioned away from the earlier 'open/planned' state (latest known status: implemented at least for the MVP).

details: Detected a status transition conflict (open -> implemented).
Committed the new milestone fact with high confidence.
Added an inference to capture effective latest status while preserving the earlier plan as non-deleted context.

repair_options: Update the stored status field from 'open' to 'implemented (MVP)' while preserving the earlier plan as historical.
Add an explicit separate field for “MVP status” = implemented, leaving the overall project status unchanged until confirmed.
Record a status timeline (planned/open -> MVP implemented) to avoid ambiguity about whether the entire project is fully complete.
